title: Aureline chronology recording-mode banner
description: Boundary schema for one recording_mode_banner_record frozen in docs/debug/chronology_and_reverse_execution_contract.md. The banner is the single source of truth the debugger UI, support-bundle preview, and AI assist read before rendering any record / replay / time-travel chrome. It carries the supported runtime / toolchain tuple, the recording-state class re-exported from the capture-session contract, the overhead and storage note shown before the user starts a recording, the privacy and export note, the closed banner-action set with typed disable reasons, and the optional chronology-mismatch state plus typed recovery action. Raw paths, raw URLs, raw command lines, raw payload bytes, raw secret material, and raw prose never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and short reviewable labels. Adding a new class to any closed vocabulary is additive-minor and bumps recording_mode_banner_schema_version; repurposing an existing token is breaking. Reverse-execution backends, time-travel debug engines, snapshot stores, and consent flows remain out of scope; the banner pins only the user-visible chronology contract every downstream surface resolves.

recording_mode_banner_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the recording-mode banner contract. Bumped only on additive-minor or breaking payload changes.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe inside debugger UI state, support bundles, and release-evidence rows. Raw absolute paths, raw URLs, raw signing material, raw secret values, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

short_label.description: Short redaction-safe label rendered verbatim by debugger UI, support-bundle preview, and AI assist. Raw paths, raw URLs, and raw command lines never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp captured when the banner record was minted.

runtime_class.description: Closed runtime / host vocabulary for the supported tuple. native_* cover OS+arch; web_browser_runtime / notebook_kernel_runtime / wasm_runtime cover non-native record/replay backends; remote_agent_runtime covers a remote agent recording lane; imported_runtime_only marks an import-only lane that cannot host live recording; unknown_runtime forces unsupported_no_backend or unsupported_runtime_segment on support_level_class.

support_level_class.description: Support-level boundary the banner declares. record_replay_supported is the only class that admits live_recording or recorded states with reverse-step support. record_only_supported can record but cannot reverse-step. profile_only_supported and live_debug_only_supported cannot host a chronology at all. import_only_supported admits imported_recording but never live_recording. unsupported_runtime_segment marks a runtime segment whose backend dropped out mid-session; unsupported_no_backend marks a runtime where no record/replay backend ships at all.

supported_runtime.description: Supported runtime / toolchain tuple plus the support-level class. The labels are rendered verbatim by the banner; the note ref points at the human-readable explanation. Raw prose never crosses this boundary.

recording_state_class.description: Recording-state vocabulary, re-exported verbatim from schemas/performance/capture_session.schema.json so the banner cannot drift from the capture-session truth. live_recording and recorded require a non-null capture_session_ref whose capture_class is replay_capture. imported_recording marks an import-only lane. recording_unavailable, expired, and artifact_mismatch each pair with a chronology_mismatch_state_class on the banner.

overhead_class.description: Overhead band re-exported from schemas/performance/capture_session.schema.json. The banner displays this value before the user starts a recording so the cost is visible up-front.

storage_band_class.description: Storage band re-exported from schemas/performance/capture_session.schema.json. Rendered next to the overhead class on the banner.

overhead_storage.description: Overhead and storage note rendered before start_recording is offered. Raw payload bytes never cross this boundary; the size hint is an optional integer for sizing UI affordances.

export_posture_class.description: Export posture for the recording bytes. local_only_no_export disables export and share actions. summary_export_safe admits manifest-with-digests exports without raw bytes. raw_export_user_consent_required and raw_export_admin_only force opt_in_required = true. raw_export_blocked_by_policy disables export with policy_disabled.

privacy_export_note.description: Privacy and export note. The redaction profile is cited by ref (the capture-session contract owns the profile body); the export posture is one of the five closed classes; opt_in_required is the boolean the banner uses to gate raw export and share actions.

banner_action_class.description: Closed banner-action vocabulary. The banner_actions array enumerates which actions are available, which are disabled with a typed reason, and which are inadmissible on this lane (so the user always sees the full set rather than guessing which buttons exist).

action_state_class.description: State of one banner action. enabled admits the action; disabled_with_reason renders the action with a typed reason; hidden_inadmissible removes the action from the rendered set (e.g. share_read_only_session on a not_shareable lane).

action_disable_reason_class.description: Closed reason vocabulary for action_state_class != enabled. Renderers cite the typed reason verbatim instead of inventing freeform copy.
Required non-null whenever action_state_class is disabled_with_reason or hidden_inadmissible. MUST be null when action_state_class is enabled.

chronology_mismatch_state_class.description: Closed chronology-mismatch state vocabulary frozen by the UI/UX spec. Each value is a stable banner row with copy and shortcuts; the banner does not collapse two states into a generic 'no chronology' string.

chronology_mismatch_recovery_action_clas.description: Closed recovery-action vocabulary paired with chronology_mismatch_state_class. Required non-null whenever chronology_mismatch_state_class is non-null; the banner never names a mismatch state without naming a recovery action.

debug_session_ref.description: Opaque ref to the debug_session_record this banner sits inside. Required non-null; the banner is a debug-session surface and cannot exist outside one.

capture_session_ref.description: Opaque ref to the performance_capture_session_record carrying the recording state, the replay capability, and the allowed verbs. Required non-null when recording_state_class is live_recording, recorded, imported_recording, expired, or artifact_mismatch. May be null when recording_state_class is not_recording or recording_unavailable.

debug_artifact_manifest_ref.description: Opaque ref to the debug_artifact_manifest_record entry whose mismatch token resolves the banner's chronology_mismatch_state_class = artifact_mismatch claim. Required non-null when chronology_mismatch_state_class is artifact_mismatch; null otherwise.

banner_actions.description: Ordered banner-action list. The list MUST enumerate every action the banner offers — even disabled ones — so the user sees a typed reason instead of a vanished button.

summary.description: Optional reviewer-facing sentence the debugger UI, support-bundle preview, and AI assist may render verbatim alongside the typed banner.
